protocol_doc: JOURNAL.md entry '2026-07-11 -- autonomous planner-improvement research loop (protocol)'
objective: improve the FB planner (policy_fb.py / decompose.py), measured as win/draw/loss vs a FIXED Stockfish skill level, using experiments/experiment_report.py for every comparison
hard_constraints: every experiment_report.py run enforces the leakage audit gate automatically -- never bypass, never train on eval_cp/stockfish-derived signal
every arena run's opponent string (e.g. 'sf:skill=3') is recorded in the JSON record automatically -- always read strength FROM the record, never assume
ALWAYS cp the current best checkpoint to a step-numbered backup file BEFORE launching the next training round -- learned the hard way in round 1 (lost the step-30000 weights, see history)

prefer NEW checkpoint filenames over overwriting data/derived/lichess_fb.pt when starting a genuinely new experimental branch (e.g. a new data scale) -- keeps every branch comparable without relying on manual backups
epochs_per_step_increment_note: 1GB shard (11.07M positions): 1 epoch = ~21615 steps, step 90000 reached ~4.16 epochs (diagnosed as a dominant cause of decline, round 2). 4GB shard (55.82M positions, batch=512): 1 epoch = ~109021 steps -- step 30000 was only ~0.275 epochs, lots of headroom

checkpoint_backups.note: round-1 output on 1GB shard, REGRESSED vs step-30000 (pre-LR-fix). Kept for comparison only.
round-2 output on 1GB shard (post-LR-fix). Partial recovery but still below step-30000. Kept for comparison only, NOT promoted to best.

round-3 output: fresh run on the 4GB shard. Beats round-1/2 on every metric, exceeds the 1GB-step30000 baseline on decompose MEAN_GAIN (0.462 vs 0.417), close-but-below on FRAC_IMPROVED/reach_slope/tau_exec. Promising but not a clean win yet -- extending further (round 4) to see if more exposure on the larger, still-fresh dataset clearly surpasses the baseline.

best.note: CONFIRMED 2026-07-11: n=40 vs sf:skill=0, +1=18-21, score=0.250, e=11666.43, REJECT at alpha=0.05. This is now the best-known (checkpoint, readout) pair by the objective metric (arena win rate), a 2.5x improvement over the flat 0.100 that persisted across 4 training-lever-only rounds using FBBoardPolicy(depth=2). Same weights as round 4's checkpoint -- the entire gain came from readout strategy, not training. `best` now tracks (checkpoint, readout) jointly since readout is confirmed to matter as much as weights for the objective metric.

current_round.action: trained combined candidate (--quasimetric --winner-pov-only, fresh, 60000 steps) = data/derived/lichess_fb_4gb_qm_wpov.pt. Evaluated via ACPL n=400 (best checkpoint so far) and KRRvKBP n=20 (fewer blunders, zero losses, more conversions, but plan-vs-search still not significant). Scaling KRRvKBP to n=60 (fresh, tablebase-verified set) for real power on the plan-persistence question -- result pending.
current_round.acpl_result: combined vs baseline: -72.8cp, 95% CI=[-99.3,-46.0], p=5.5e-7 (best checkpoint so far). combined vs winner-pov-only alone: -31.1cp, 95% CI=[-57.6,-5.0], p=0.066 -- quasimetric's SPECIFIC incremental contribution is borderline on this metric. artifacts/experiments/acpl_comparison_n400_round12.json.
current_round.krrkbp_n20_result: single-policy scan: terminations improved (INSUFFICIENT_MATERIAL 9->8, THREEFOLD_REPETITION 7->11, CHECKMATE 2(1W1L)->1(1W0L) -- zero losses now). Paired FBSearchPolicy-vs-FBPlanPolicy: both readouts convert more wins (3->4/20, 1->3/20) but e=0.38, not significant -- same underpowered-at-n=20 pattern as round 10.
current_round.krrkbp_n60_result: generated + tablebase-verified (all WDL=2) a fresh 60-position set, artifacts/experiments/krrkbp_fixed_set_n60.json, seed=123. FBSearchPolicy=0.583 vs FBPlanPolicy=0.625, mean_diff=+0.042, CI=[-0.589,+0.672], e=0.47 -- STILL not significant at 3x the sample (sign even flipped vs n=20, consistent with noise not signal). Real pattern found: FBPlanPolicy lost twice (0-1) across n=20+n=60=80 total games; FBSearchPolicy never lost once. Plan-persistence trades a few more conversions for occasional walked-into refutations -- a wash, not a win, at current hyperparameters.
current_round.status_summary: SUPERSEDED by round 13 (see history/current_round_13) -- Kaveh asked for search-depth sensitivity + ply-gap calibration + full self-play infra, all built. Overnight autonomous loop in progress, do not stop unless explicitly told or genuinely stuck.
current_round.acpl_methodology_note: acpl_probe.py had a bug (mate_score=100000 let rare forced-mate detections dominate the mean with near-lottery-sized outliers) -- fixed to mate_score=1000 (standard ACPL convention). Always use n>=400 for this probe; n=100 is underpowered to distinguish real deltas from noise (see round 11 history).
current_round.opponent_ladder_note: the original full-board-vs-graduated-Stockfish opponent_ladder below is SUPERSEDED by the KRRvKBP diagnostic phase (see phase_2_krrkbp_diagnostic) and the ACPL probe as the primary fast metrics; re-activate the ladder only if/when returning to full-board arena evaluation.

current_round_16.action: asym_weight 0.015 (ONE change from round 15's rejected 0.05): training lichess_fb_4gb_qm_asym015.pt, same 3-part gate.
current_round_16.round_15_verdict: REJECTED as configured (weight 0.05): gate 1 PASS dramatic (frac(rev<=fwd) 0.030 vs 0.27-0.35 before, gap +0.325), gate 2 borderline FAIL (nearest-exemplar rho +0.123 < +0.15), gate 3 clear FAIL (ACPL +30.9cp worse, CI [+4.7,+57.1], p=0.01). Mechanism understood: over-weighted auxiliary loss traded k=1 retrieval (0.97->0.79, ACPL's whole diet) for long-horizon structure -- k=20-50 cliff moved OUTWARD for the first time (0.69->0.77, 0.23->0.30). Tuning failure, not mechanism failure.

current_round_15.action: asymmetry-margin lever (ONE lever): trained lichess_fb_4gb_qm_asym.pt -- quasimetric + ply-gap 0.05 + asym-weight 0.05/margin 0.2 + gen2-mix 0.3, fresh 90k steps. Material-drop pairs (capture crossed) hinge d(reverse) > d(forward) + margin; derived from trajectory direction only.
current_round_15.status: closed -- rejected as configured, see current_round_16.round_15_verdict
current_round_15.pre_registered_gates: on the fitness probe: asymmetry frac(rev<=fwd) must drop from ~0.27-0.35 toward <= 0.10 AND nearest-exemplar KRvK rho must not degrade below +0.15 AND ACPL n=400 paired vs incumbent must not significantly worsen. All three or the lever is rejected.
current_round_15.round_14_closeout: goal-as-region READOUT rejected at play in 3 decisive paired tests (hard-max on gen2: e=65; hard-max on incumbent: e=2.8e7; soft-min tau=0.1 on incumbent: e=21811 -- all REJECT, centroid wins). Diagnosis retained: centroids flat vs true plies-to-mate, nearest-exemplar rho real (+0.165 incumbent, +0.252 gen2 -- endgame curriculum DID improve the geometry). Readout can't fix representation sparsity; region goals shelved until embedding calibrates better in those regions. Instruments kept (goal_bank.py, krrkbp_arena --compare bank, bank-capable policies + tests).

current_round_14.action: endgame-start curriculum: gen2 self-play (500 games, endgame_start_frac=0.5, incumbent ckpt) -> retrain quasimetric+ply-gap+gen2-mix -> qm_fitness_probe. PRE-REGISTERED SUCCESS CRITERION: KRvK Spearman rho(d, plies-to-mate) >= +0.3 (currently ~0.0 flat on BOTH checkpoints).
current_round_14.round_13_verdict: NO PROMOTION. gen1 (quasimetric+ply-gap+selfplay, no winner-pov): ACPL +13.9cp worse than incumbent (CI [-11.0,+40.3], p=0.15, statistical wash); KRRvKBP 0.475/0.450 vs incumbent's 0.583/0.625; but structurally better (spread 2.35 vs 1.79, eff rank +2, k=50 retrieval 0.29 vs 0.23, best DIFF_SLOPE separation +0.208/-0.050). Three levers changed at once -- per-lever attribution impossible, accepted to unblock pipeline.
current_round_14.steering_finding: qm_fitness_probe (built this round from lit survey: PQE vio, Syzygy calibration, asymmetry audit, horizon retrieval, degeneracy panel): metric STRUCTURE is healthy on both ckpts (zero triangle violations on d, no collapse) but distance-to-mate calibration is FLAT (KRvK rho ~0 from mate-in-1 to mate-in-31 -- pawnless KRvK DTZ == true plies-to-mate, the clean ground truth; KRRvKBP DTZ compresses via captures, probe fixed accordingly). Root cause: OOD coverage -- human games never visit these regions. Retrieval cliff at k=20-50 plies and weak asymmetry gap (frac_rev<=fwd 0.27-0.35, want ~0) are targets 2 and 3.

current_round_13.action: CORRECTED RELAUNCH after Fable review (see JOURNAL.md 2026-07-12 13:20): training data/derived/lichess_fb_4gb_qm_gen1.pt -- quasimetric + ply-gap 0.05 + selfplay-mix 0.3 (data/selfplay/gen1, 400 games by lichess_fb_4gb_qm_wpov.pt), explicit --shards prefix4gb, NO winner-pov (removed from the codebase entirely), 90000 steps fresh, python -u.
current_round_13.review_findings: first launch spun at step 0 for 35 min from TWO stacked bugs: (1) newest_shard_dir() resolved to data/shards/selfplay_gen1 (newest mtime), so the 'human' source was the 30k-position self-play dir, not the 55.8M-position 4GB prefix -- self-play output now lives under data/selfplay/ and selfplay_generate.py refuses to write under data/shards/; (2) winner-pov kept a measured 68.8/512 rows on that (mostly-drawn) data, below the batch//2=256 skip guard -- pass rate 0.000, infinite spin. Winner-pov is REMOVED (Kaveh): losing trajectories carry the bad-future signal ply-gap calibration needs. Round-11's evidence stands; the mechanism is retired.

node_sweep_result.note: 200/800 flat (clean, both sampled from the human prefix). The 2000 stage started after selfplay_gen1/ existed and sampled ITS positions from self-play shards -- not comparable, re-run queued post-training with explicit --shards. Direction still holds on the clean pair: more search alone does not fix tactical blindness.

current_round_13.bug_fixed: val_metrics() was computing printed VAL_TOP1/TOP8/loss via raw f@b.T, bypassing score_matrix() -- quasimetric runs' printed validation diagnostics were wrong (actual trained weights unaffected, only the human-readable log). Fixed. Also: self-play shards now stamp odd game_ids (holdout rule silently ate ids % 50 == 0).
current_round_13.known_caveat: planner/decompose.py (hop_reach, waypoint_scores) and the viz builders score with raw F@z dot products -- mis-calibrated for quasimetric checkpoints. Nothing measured so far is affected (ACPL/KRRvKBP go through fb.score). Thread fb.score through before trusting decompose/viz numbers on quasimetric runs.
current_round_13.instruction: Kaveh (2026-07-12, will be away for many hours): 'keep progressing autonomously. don't stop... build all the self-play stuff and everything that's on our roadmap.' Plus: commit work periodically. Continue the PI-refinement cycle (generate self-play with current best -> train -> evaluate via ACPL n=400 + KRRvKBP n=60 -> promote if improved -> generate more self-play with new best -> repeat) autonomously. Journal every round with timestamps. Only stop the loop if explicitly told, or if genuinely stuck after trying the stuck_playbook (write the conclusion clearly first).

phase_2_krrkbp_diagnostic.why: full-board vs graduated Stockfish makes planner failures hard to diagnose ('too many [concepts] and I don't know how to diagnose the planner's failures' -- Kaveh). Switched to a narrow, interpretable, provably-winning endgame instead.
phase_2_krrkbp_diagnostic.scenario: White K+R+R vs Black K+B(light-squared)+P(e-file, rank 3-6), Stockfish always plays Black (fixed colors)
phase_2_krrkbp_diagnostic.fixed_set: artifacts/experiments/krrkbp_fixed_set.json (20 positions, seed=42), all confirmed WDL=2 (winning for White) via Syzygy tablebases in data/syzygy/
phase_2_krrkbp_diagnostic.tablebase_role: OBSERVATIONAL OVERLAY ONLY, not a scoring signal -- win/draw/loss vs Stockfish remains the objective metric; DTZ is for the decision-viewer overlay so Kaveh can compare the planner's actual play to optimal distance-to-mate when inspecting visually. 'if it wins some other way, who am I to penalize it?' -- Kaveh.
phase_2_krrkbp_diagnostic.params_fixed_for_now: node budget, beam, Stockfish strength -- Kaveh's call ('we escalate later. let's fix params for now, and see if we can tune the algo'). Escalation (harder Stockfish / larger budget) deferred until an algorithm win is found on this scenario.
phase_2_krrkbp_diagnostic.search_redesign: FBSearchPolicy now takes max_nodes (node budget) instead of fixed ply-depth, deriving depth per-move from real branching factor -- modeled on Leela Chess Zero's node economy (~150-200 nodes target, ~10x below its ~1500-2000 'actually playing' reference point), so any win has to come from the plan, not from out-searching the opponent.

history.operational_mistake: train_lichess_fb.py overwrote data/derived/lichess_fb.pt in place; step-30000 weights were never backed up first and are now unrecoverable. Fixed going forward.
history.diagnosis: no LR schedule -- constant lr=3e-4 for 30000 steps (~1.4 epochs) past the point where it stopped helping. WebSearched: constant/non-decaying LR is a known risk factor for representation drift in InfoNCE-style contrastive training; SimCLR/CLIP both cosine-decay to ~1/10 of peak LR.
history.fix: added cosine LR decay to train_lichess_fb.py (lr -> lr/10 over each invocation's remaining steps).

history.action: train 60000->90000, cosine LR decay to lr/10 (the round-1 fix applied)
history.result: PARTIAL RECOVERY, STILL BELOW BASELINE -- fix was insufficient alone

evidence.reach_slope_lost: 0.263 -> 0.072 (CONTINUED DECLINING, now near zero; step-30000 was 0.490)

history.diagnosis: LR schedule helped (decompose partial recovery) but reach_slope/tau_exec kept monotonically declining across BOTH rounds regardless -- the LR fix addressed part of the problem but not all of it. Step 90000 = ~4.16 epochs over the SAME 1GB/11.07M-position shard (the original successful 30000-step run only reached ~1.4 epochs). Epoch-repetition/overfitting on a fixed contrastive dataset is the leading remaining hypothesis: the model may be starting to fit shard-specific structure rather than generalizable reach geometry.
history.decision: 2 non-improving rounds at the same lever (constant-shard training) = stuck per protocol. Rather than a 3rd same-data extension, testing the data-scale hypothesis directly: downloaded a 4GB Lichess prefix (4x the position count) and launched a FRESH 30000-step run on it (round 3), matching the ORIGINAL successful run's step budget but with ~4x more unique positions to draw from.

history.action: download 4GB Lichess prefix (55.82M positions, ~5x the 1GB shard), fresh 30000-step train on it
history.result: PROMISING -- beats rounds 1&2 on every metric, exceeds the 1GB baseline on MEAN_GAIN, close-but-below on the rest

evidence.arena_vs_sf_skill0: 0.100, +1=6-33 (still losing badly in absolute terms, but the best of the three post-1GB-30k checkpoints: 0.075 -> 0.087 -> 0.100)

history.diagnosis: at 30000 steps this is only ~0.275 epochs over the 4GB shard -- clearly better than the 1.4+ epoch 1GB extensions, and the epoch-repetition hypothesis looks directionally correct, but 30000 steps alone didn't CLEANLY beat the 1GB-30000 baseline either (still slightly below on FRAC_IMPROVED/reach_slope/tau_exec). Considered whether this refutes data-scale as a lever, but rejected that reading: 5x more unique positions at the SAME step count means proportionally less exposure per position (0.275 avg passes vs 1.4) -- a fair test needs comparable gradient exposure, not just a comparable step count.
history.decision: extend training on the SAME 4GB-shard checkpoint to 60000 steps (round 4, still only ~0.55 epochs -- lots of headroom before the 1.4-4-epoch danger zone found on the 1GB shard) rather than treating 30000 steps as the final word on the data-scale hypothesis.

history.result: MIXED, no clean win -- but the decisive finding is that arena score has been FLAT across all 4 training-lever rounds
history.diagnosis: the embedding-quality proxies (decompose, reach-slope) and the actual arena win rate have decoupled -- 4 rounds of tuning training volume never touched arena score at all, even while some proxies improved. This matches arena_real.py's own documented expectation (no-search greedy readout losing to Stockfish is EXPECTED) and confirms via WebSearch: pure imitation learning has a known ceiling without self-play, and combining a learned value function with deeper lookahead is the literature's practical unlock ahead of a full self-play retraining loop.
history.decision: PIVOT from training-volume levers to an architecture lever: implemented FBSearchPolicy (catspace/nn/policy_fb.py), beam-limited multi-ply minimax over the SAME F(s)@z value function, no retraining needed. Caught and fixed a real mate-scoring bug via a dedicated correctness test (flat MATE_SCORE let the search settle for a slower-than-necessary forced mate; fixed via ply-distance discounting). Wired into experiment_report.py as opt-in --search-depth/--search-beam. First test (n=16, depth=3 beam=4) scored 0.250, up from the flat 0.100 -- see round 5.

history.action: first FBSearchPolicy arena test: depth=3 beam=4, n=16 games, max-plies=60
history.result: PROMISING FIRST READ -- 0.250 score (+1=6-9), up from the flat 0.100 baseline across 4 prior rounds

evidence.note: e=9.50 needs e>=20 to reject H0 at alpha=0.05 -- real signal but n=16 is too small to call this confirmed yet

history.diagnosis: depth=4/beam=6 measured ~20s/move (too slow for a real arena run, 9-18h for 40 games); depth=3/beam=4 measured ~1.8s/move, tractable (~40s/game observed). This is the FIRST movement at all on the arena-score axis across 6 rounds total.
history.decision: run the protocol-standard n=40 confirmation at the same depth=3/beam=4 config before treating this as validated (round 6, in progress).

history.action: confirmation run: FBSearchPolicy depth=3 beam=4, n=40 games vs sf:skill=0
history.result: CONFIRMED -- 0.250 score, statistically significant, exactly reproduces the n=16 read

evidence.note: identical score_mean (0.250) at both n=16 and n=40 -- unusually clean reproduction, not just directionally consistent

history.diagnosis: the first architecture lever tried (as opposed to 6 rounds total of mostly training-volume levers) produced the first STATISTICALLY CONFIRMED improvement on the objective metric in the whole loop. Promoted to `best`.
history.decision: run a depth=2/beam=8 control (round 7, in progress) to isolate whether the gain is from the extra ply specifically, before exploring deeper configs or making FBSearchPolicy the standard readout for all future rounds.

history.action: control run: FBSearchPolicy depth=2 beam=8 (same ply-depth as the old flat baseline, new framework)
history.result: PARTIAL ATTRIBUTION -- 0.163, above the flat 0.100 baseline but below depth=3's 0.250

evidence.comparison: 0.100 (old FBBoardPolicy depth=2) -> 0.163 (new framework, same depth=2) -> 0.250 (new framework, depth=3)

history.diagnosis: the gain cleanly decomposes into two real, both-significant contributions: ~42% (0.063 of 0.150 total) comes from the new beam-search framework ITSELF at equal ply-depth (most likely the ply-distance mate-discount and/or how beam-based opponent-reply ranking differs from FBBoardPolicy's exhaustive MIN, even though beam=8 considers FEWER opponent replies than FBBoardPolicy's unrestricted enumeration in high-branching positions) -- ~58% (0.087 of 0.150) comes specifically from the extra ply (depth 2->3). Both factors are real; this is not simply 'more plies = better', but depth clearly matters too.
history.decision: push to depth=4 to see if the trend continues (round 8, timing narrower beams first since depth=4/beam=6 was measured too slow at ~20s/move).

history.action: arena test: FBSearchPolicy depth=4 beam=3, n=40 vs sf:skill=0
history.result: REGRESSION -- 0.200, breaks the depth=2(0.163)->depth=3(0.250) trend rather than continuing it
history.diagnosis: depth=4 had to shrink beam to 3 (from depth=3's beam=4) to stay tractable -- not a clean 'more plies, same beam' comparison. Narrowing the beam to buy depth lost more than the extra ply gained: diminishing/negative returns on ply-depth alone at a fixed embedding.
history.decision: closes out the ply-depth-tuning-vs-full-board-Stockfish research thread. FBSearchPolicy(depth=3, beam=4) remains `best`. PIVOTED to (1) node-budget search (max_nodes replacing fixed depth) and (2) the KRRvKBP diagnostic scenario -- see phase_2_krrkbp_diagnostic and JOURNAL.md '2026-07-11 18:52'.

history.action: test --winner-pov-only outcome-conditioned training vs a step-matched plain-continuation control (both 60000->90000 steps), via ACPL blunder probe
history.result: CONFIRMED -- winner-pov-only significantly beats the control, not just the baseline
history.diagnosis: outcome-conditioning (filtering (anchor,goal) pairs to the eventual winner's POV, using `result` which was already flowing through the pipeline unused) produces a real effect beyond just more training steps. Effect size modest (~22cp) but statistically unambiguous with proper power (n=400 paired, both Wilcoxon and bootstrap CI agree). A first n=100 read looked promising but was NOT significant on the control comparison (p=0.43) -- caught by scaling up before concluding.
history.methodology_bug_caught_and_fixed: acpl_probe.py's mate_score=100000 let rare forced-mate detections dominate the MEAN with near-lottery-sized outliers (ACPL jumped from ~300 to ~1000-1600 at n=400, nothing to do with the checkpoints). Fixed to mate_score=1000 (standard ACPL convention).
history.decision: adopt --winner-pov-only as the default going forward. Combine with the (separately implemented and unit-tested) quasimetric TorchFB mode for round 12 -- see current_round.

stuck_playbook: 1. re-read JOURNAL.md's decompose/eval-head findings for an unexploited lever (e.g. the MC-rollout executability verifier flagged 2026-07-11 as the next priority)

2. WebSearch/deep-research for techniques: self-play fine-tuning after imitation bootstrap, PI (policy-iteration) refinement loops, contrastive-embedding improvements for game-playing agents, curriculum/data-quality filtering

3. try a hyperparameter/data lever instead of raw step count: lr schedule (DONE round 1->2, insufficient alone), gamma (pairing horizon), embedding dim d, FBBoardPolicy depth 1 vs 2, DATA SCALE (IN PROGRESS round 3 -- epoch-repetition hypothesis)

4. if genuinely stuck after research + multiple alternate levers, write up the dead end honestly in JOURNAL.md (a documented negative result is a valid research outcome) and move to the next lever rather than looping forever on one idea
